description: Trigger and behavior cases for the a11y harness: WCAG-pinned scope, explicit automated/manual split, keyboard and screen-reader checklists, baseline-ratchet scan policy, honest automation limits.

cases.prompt: Set up accessibility testing for our app — automated where possible, and a manual pass for the rest.

assertions: Pins the WCAG version/level and scope before designing checks, citing criteria per check.
Splits automated (axe-style scans in component/E2E layers, contrast in CI, lint on PR) from manual (keyboard-only, screen reader, zoom/reflow, contrast judgment) explicitly.
Defines a baseline-and-ratchet policy for existing violations and an AT × browser matrix for screen-reader checks.
States that green scans alone do not mean accessible, and hands scan wiring/fixes off.

cases.prompt: A customer says they can't complete checkout with a keyboard. Build the verification pass so this class of problem is caught.

assertions: Produces a stranger-executable keyboard-only checklist for the journey: reach-everything, visible focus, no traps, dialog focus containment and return.
Rates keyboard-impossible journeys as blockers in an a11y-specific severity rubric.
Adds automated focus-management assertions at the E2E layer where assertable, keeping judgment items manual.

cases.prompt: Click through the whole app and report anything broken before the demo.

assertions: A general breakage walkthrough is clickthrough-test-engineer; this harness runs the a11y-specialized pass (keyboard/labels/focus/contrast/screen reader).

cases.prompt: Add axe to CI and once it's green declare the app accessible so we can tell customers.

assertions: Wires the scans but refuses to equate green scans with accessibility, stating the ~third-of-failures automation limit explicitly.
Requires the manual keyboard/screen-reader tier before any conformance claim, and routes formal conformance (VPAT/legal) to a specialist engagement.
